_comment_field_path_drop_patterns: Case-insensitive fnmatch glob patterns matched against a ChangeEntry's top-level `path` string (comprehensive.json records[].changes[].path). Used by drop_rules whose require_all_changes_drop is true: a bundle only drops this way if EVERY non-suppressed change path across its non-context members matches one of these.
_comment_narrative_signal_patterns: Case-insensitive fnmatch glob patterns matched against a ChangeEntry's top-level `path` string. Used by drop_rules whose require_no_narrative_change is true: if ANY non-suppressed change path matches one of these, that disqualifies the bundle from being dropped (a name/description/value change is player-visible, not pure plumbing).

deep_rules.description: OMOD content whose EDID marks it a unique/legendary item mod (mod_Custom_*, *_mod_Custom_*, mod_Legendary_*) is always worth a full writeup.
POST_-prefixed EDIDs mark deferred/not-yet-released (datamined) content on a record actually in the diff (not merely context) -- always flagged for a human/agent look, regardless of record type.
A brand-new WEAP/QUST/NPC_/PERK/ALCH/COBJ is always a deep-writeup candidate -- new obtainable content, not a tweak.

A changed record of a gameplay-major type with at least one real NUMERIC stat delta (a scalar/curve-table change, not just any field touch). Deliberately tighter than 'any non-rename change': a HAZD bundle whose only change is one unmapped flag bit (a hex-string bitmask, not a number) or a BOOK's holotape text edit does NOT qualify here -- those fall through to ambiguous, where the assessor can still promote them if they actually matter. Bundles whose ONLY change is an EditorID rename don't qualify either -- those route to brief_rules/renamed_or_cut_only instead. See triage_bundles.py's is_numeric_change_entry for the exact predicate.

brief_rules.description: Every non-context member of the bundle is a brand-new record (of a type not already claimed by a deep_rules added rule) -- existence is the whole story, e.g. added cosmetic-type records (ARTO/SNDR/AVTR/ENTM).
Every non-context member of the bundle was removed this patch.

Every non-context member is 'changed' status, has an EditorID rename and/or a cut/deprecation marker classification, and has NO other substantive field delta -- a pure rename/vaulting, e.g. a cut-rename (zzz_/CUT_/DEL_ prefix added this patch) with nothing else touched.

drop_rules.description: Bundle is nothing but placed-object-reference (REFR) placement churn -- world positioning, not gameplay content -- regardless of status.

Bundle is nothing but SNDR/MSWP/PLYT/STMP/TRNS/ARTO/IMAD tweaks (sound/material-swap/playlist/stamp/transform/art-object/image-space-adapter) with no name/description/value change anywhere -- purely technical/cosmetic plumbing.

Every non-suppressed field change across the bundle's non-context 'changed' members matches a data-driven drop pattern (top-level field_path_drop_patterns: model/material/texture paths, previs/precombine flags, LCTN 'Master * References' arrays, REFR placement transforms). Bundles with zero recorded changes do NOT match this rule (avoids treating an empty/vacuous diff as drop-worthy noise instead of leaving it to the rename/cut brief rule or ambiguous).
